summary: Exceptionally well-structured test suite that covers all task requirements across 8 explicitly labelled TDD cycles. Every major function (Read-DependencyManifest for both formats, Read-LicenseConfig, Get-DependencyLicense, Test-LicenseCompliance, New-ComplianceReport, Export-ComplianceReport) has its own Describe block with multiple It cases. Edge-case coverage is strong: null and throwing lookups return UNKNOWN, case-insensitive list matching, deny-list priority over allow-list, empty manifests, empty lists, malformed JSON, non-existent files, unsupported formats, mixed-status summary counts, and metadata (timestamp, path) on the report. Fixtures are cleanly referenced via BeforeAll-scoped variables, temp files are cleaned up in try/finally, and mock scriptblocks capture arguments for assertion. Minor gaps keep rigor from a perfect score: the config file itself is never tested for malformed JSON; Export-ComplianceReport has only two shallow tests (no denied/unknown entries in the serialized output, no CSV/other-format negative tests); and requirements.txt edge cases like pin-less entries or extras notation are not probed. Overall this is a trustworthy, regression-catching suite that a team could confidently rely on.
